models.generation_context: Fourth-generation Continental GT (4G), introduced 2024. All 2026 trims share the new plug-in hybrid V8 powertrain (4.0L twin-turbo V8 + electric motor). Available in two power tunes: High Performance Hybrid (671 bhp) on base/S/Azure trims, and Ultra Performance Hybrid (771 bhp) on Speed and Mulliner trims. The previous W12 was discontinued with the 3rd-generation Continental GT.
models.model_summary: Bentley's 2+2 grand-touring coupe, sold exclusively as a plug-in hybrid for 2026. Pairs a twin-turbocharged 4.0L V8 with an integrated electric motor and a 25.9 kWh battery delivering up to 50 miles (WLTP) of pure-electric range. Five trim levels are offered, distinguished by power output (671 hp on base/S/Azure vs 771 hp on Speed/Mulliner), exterior styling (Blackline on S, chrome jewelry on Azure, body-color carbon-fiber on Mulliner), interior treatments, and wheel choices.

reliability.summary: JD Power Vehicle Dependability Study does not meaningfully sample Bentley due to very low US sales volume, and Consumer Reports does not publish a predicted-reliability score for the Continental GT. No quantitative reliability rating is available.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power APEAL does not publish scores for Bentley because of low US sales volume. Owner sentiment in press coverage is consistently very positive on craftsmanship and ride quality, but no quantified APEAL score exists.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the 4th-generation Continental GT for transformative chassis revisions: dual-valve dampers, twin-chamber air springs, and the upgraded 48V Dynamic Ride active anti-roll system dramatically reduce body roll and sharpen turn-in compared to the prior generation. The plug-in hybrid V8 is described as seamless and breathtakingly quick, with the 771 hp Speed/Mulliner variants reaching 60 mph in 3.1 seconds. Common critiques focus on the 5,400-lb curb weight that defines the car's character, the still-firm low-speed ride on the largest wheel options, and prices that ascend rapidly with bespoke options.

owner_reviews.summary: Edmunds and KBB do not publish meaningful owner-review aggregates for the Continental GT due to the small US owner population and limited consumer submissions. No quantitative star rating is available.

Five drive modes (Bentley/Sport/Comfort/Custom/EV)
City Specification ADAS package standard
Wireless Apple CarPlay and Android Auto
Standard 21-inch ten-spoke alloy wheels

trims.notes: Sole-trim family: Continental GT base is the entry point to the model. is_base_trim:true with delta_from_base:null per sole-trim atomic rule. Bentley does not publish US MSRP on consumer or press sites; per spec §13 and §4 dealer sites, content farms (Carbuzz, Motor1, Autoblog, Autoevolution), and KBB/Edmunds retail prices are forbidden as primary MSRP source - msrp_base and destination_fee set to null with documentation. EPA fueleconomy.gov ID 49752 verified as 2026 Bentley Continental GT plug-in hybrid; EPA shows combined gas MPG only (city/highway split not separately published for the PHEV - 19 applied to all three slots). High Performance Hybrid powertrain produces 671 bhp combined (512 bhp V8 + 188 bhp e-motor). NHTSA and IIHS have not crash-tested Bentley Continental GT. Plug type CCS1 standard for US market. EV-only top speed limited to 87 mph. MSRP source: MotorTrend editorial spec page (per §4.6 scoped policy; manufacturer does not publish US MSRP).

Sports exhaust system
Precision Design full-LED matrix headlamps
Dinamica technical fabric on steering wheel/gear lever
Dark-tinted taillamps and quad oval exhaust outlets
Piano Black veneer standard

trims.notes: Sole-trim family: Continental GT S. is_base_trim:true with delta_from_base:null per sole-trim atomic rule. Shares High Performance Hybrid powertrain with base/Azure but with Bentley Performance Active Chassis (previously Speed-only) including stiffer dampers, retuned springs, dynamic torque vectoring and electronic limited-slip differential, plus distinct Blackline exterior treatment with gloss black matrix grilles. Top speed 191 mph vs 168 mph for base/Azure - same engine, different gearing/aero limit. EPA fueleconomy.gov ID 49752 shared with Continental GT base since EPA does not test or publish separate listings for individual trim levels within the same powertrain configuration (per spec §4 EPA-unavailable rule). Bentley does not publish US MSRP. NHTSA/IIHS untested. Press release T0420056EN October 2025 announced both GT S and GTC S simultaneously. MSRP not findable from allowed editorial sources as of 2026-05-15.

trims.notes: Sole-trim family: Continental GT Speed. is_base_trim:true with delta_from_base:null per sole-trim atomic rule and per spec §13 separate-powertrain-line rule (Ultra Performance Hybrid 771 bhp is a higher-output engine tune distinct from High Performance Hybrid 671 bhp on base/S/Azure). Most powerful production Bentley ever made; 0-60 in 3.1 sec, top speed 208 mph (requires summer tires). Touring Specification (HUD, predictive ACC, night vision) standard. Carbon ceramic brakes standard. EPA fueleconomy.gov ID 49752 cited per spec §4 EPA-unavailable rule - EPA does not publish separate Speed trim entries; the 25.9 kWh battery and PHEV system architecture are shared across all Continental GT trims. Bentley does not publish US MSRP. NHTSA/IIHS untested per spec §13. MSRP source: MotorTrend editorial spec page (per §4.6 scoped policy; manufacturer does not publish US MSRP).

trims.notes: Sole-trim family: Continental GT Mulliner. is_base_trim:true with delta_from_base:null per sole-trim atomic rule. Mulliner has its own model card on bentleymotors.com (not a package per spec §6.5), with unique 22-inch self-leveling wheels with floating Bentley wing center, unique diamond-in-diamond leather quilting pattern, unique two-tone veneers, and integrated Mulliner spec badging differentiating it from Speed. Shares Ultra Performance Hybrid powertrain with Speed (771 bhp, 0-60 in 3.1 sec, top 208 mph) but positions as the ultra-luxury rather than performance flagship - Touring Spec standard. EPA fueleconomy.gov ID 49752 shared per spec §4 EPA-unavailable rule. Bentley does not publish US MSRP. NHTSA/IIHS untested. MSRP source: MotorTrend editorial spec page (per §4.6 scoped policy; manufacturer does not publish US MSRP).

trims.notes: Sole-trim family: Continental GT Azure. is_base_trim:true with delta_from_base:null per sole-trim atomic rule. Shares High Performance Hybrid powertrain (671 bhp, 3.5 sec 0-60, 168 mph top) with base/S. EPA fueleconomy.gov ID 49752 shared per spec §4 EPA-unavailable rule. Bentley does not publish US MSRP. NHTSA/IIHS untested. MSRP not findable from allowed editorial sources as of 2026-05-15.

models.notes: Continental GT is offered in five trims for 2026, all sharing the PHEV V8 powertrain platform but split across two power tunes (High Performance Hybrid at 671 bhp on base/S/Azure; Ultra Performance Hybrid at 771 bhp on Speed/Mulliner). Per spec §13 ultra-luxury rule, Bentley does not publish US MSRP on consumer or press sites; per spec §4 dealer sites, content farms, and KBB/Edmunds retail prices are forbidden as primary MSRP source - msrp_base/destination_fee null with documentation across all trims. NHTSA and IIHS have not crash-tested any Continental GT. EPA fueleconomy.gov publishes a single ID (49752) for the Continental GT line that covers all PHEV trims. Per sole-trim atomic rule, all 5 trims have their own trim_family (each with distinct wheels/grille/badging) so each is is_base_trim:true with delta_from_base:null. Excluded from this model entry: Continental GT Supersports (pre-production rear-drive performance variant, not yet on sale).

models.generation_context: Fourth-generation Continental GTC (4G convertible), introduced 2024 alongside the Coupe. Soft-top convertible variant of the Continental GT, with a Z-folding four-layer fabric roof that operates at speeds up to 30 mph. All 2026 trims share the new plug-in hybrid V8 powertrain (4.0L twin-turbo V8 + electric motor). Available in two power tunes: High Performance Hybrid (671 bhp) on base/S/Azure and Ultra Performance Hybrid (771 bhp) on Speed and Mulliner.
models.model_summary: Bentley's open-top 2+2 grand tourer, sold exclusively as a plug-in hybrid for 2026. The four-layer fabric Z-folding roof opens or closes in 19 seconds at speeds up to 30 mph. Mechanically identical to the Continental GT Coupe except for additional body bracing and a smaller (4.73 cu-ft) boot due to roof stowage and PHEV battery packaging behind the rear seats. Five trim levels mirror the Coupe lineup with convertible-specific top speeds and additional weight.

reliability.summary: JD Power VDS and Consumer Reports do not meaningfully sample Bentley due to very low US sales volume. No quantitative reliability rating is available.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power APEAL does not publish scores for Bentley because of low US sales volume.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the GTC for its remarkably stiff convertible structure, citing nearly zero cowl shake even on rough pavement, and the same dramatically improved chassis dynamics as the Coupe (4G dual-valve dampers, twin-chamber air, 48V active anti-roll). The PHEV V8 delivers near-coupe performance with the open-air experience. Critiques focus on the much smaller boot (4.73 cu-ft vs 9.2 in the coupe) due to roof stowage and PHEV battery packaging, the substantial weight gain over the Coupe, and a top speed limited to 191 mph on Speed/Mulliner vs 208 on the Coupe.

owner_reviews.summary: Edmunds and KBB do not publish meaningful owner-review aggregates for the Continental GTC due to small US owner population and limited consumer submissions.

trims.notes: Sole-trim family: Continental GTC base. is_base_trim:true with delta_from_base:null per sole-trim atomic rule. Convertible variant of Continental GT - shares powertrain, dimensions, and most equipment but with reduced 4.73 cu-ft boot (vs 9.2 for coupe) due to roof stowage and PHEV battery placement, plus ~390 lb of body bracing for open-top rigidity. EPA fueleconomy.gov ID 49753 verified specifically for 2026 Continental GTC (separate from coupe ID 49752). 0-60 in 3.8 sec is slower than the coupe's 3.5 sec due to weight; top speed limited to 168 mph. Bentley does not publish US MSRP per spec §13. NHTSA/IIHS untested. MSRP source: MotorTrend editorial spec page (per §4.6 scoped policy; manufacturer does not publish US MSRP).

EPA ID 49753 shared with all other GTC trims per spec §4 EPA-unavailable rule. Bentley does not publish US MSRP. NHTSA/IIHS untested. Announced jointly with GT S in October 2025 press release T0420056EN. MSRP not findable from allowed editorial sources as of 2026-05-15.

models.notes: Continental GTC is the open-top variant of the Continental GT - 5 trims mirroring the Coupe lineup. All five trims share the PHEV V8 platform; two power tunes (HPH 671 bhp on base/S/Azure; UPH 771 bhp on Speed/Mulliner). Per spec §13 ultra-luxury, Bentley does not publish US MSRP - msrp_base/destination_fee null across all trims. NHTSA and IIHS have not crash-tested any Continental GTC. EPA fueleconomy.gov publishes a single ID (49753) for the GTC line. All 5 trims have their own trim_family per sole-trim atomic rule. Boot volume reduced to 4.73 cu-ft (vs 9.2 in coupe) due to roof stowage and PHEV battery placement behind rear seats. Curb weight ~390 lb higher than coupe due to convertible body bracing.

models.generation_context: Third-generation Flying Spur (3G), refreshed for 2025 with all-PHEV powertrain lineup replacing the prior W12 and pure V8 options. All 2026 trims share the new plug-in hybrid V8 platform (4.0L twin-turbo V8 + 25.9 kWh battery + integrated electric motor). Available in two power tunes: High Performance Hybrid (671 bhp) on base/Azure and Ultra Performance Hybrid (771 bhp) on Speed/Mulliner.
models.model_summary: Bentley's four-door luxury sedan and now an exclusively plug-in hybrid offering, sharing its 4.0L twin-turbo V8 + electric motor architecture with the Continental GT. Four trims for 2026: base, Azure (comfort/wellness), Speed (performance), and Mulliner (ultra-luxury pinnacle). All trims deliver up to 50 miles WLTP electric-only range. Available with adjustable rear-seat comfort options including 10-inch rear entertainment screens, massage seating, and adaptive air suspension.

reliability.summary: JD Power VDS and Consumer Reports do not meaningfully sample Bentley due to very low US sales volume. No quantitative reliability rating is available.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power APEAL does not publish scores for Bentley because of low US sales volume.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the 2026 Flying Spur for the all-PHEV transition: silent electric departures, sledgehammer power on tap, and the same dramatically improved chassis dynamics as the Continental GT (twin-chamber air, dual-valve dampers, 48V active anti-roll). The Mulliner and Speed variants are now equally powerful at 771 bhp, with Speed positioned as the dynamic flagship and Mulliner as the ultimate luxury expression. Critiques focus on the considerable weight gain due to PHEV battery (now ~5,820 lb curb weight), the smaller-than-segment trunk (12.2 cu-ft), and prices that climb steeply with bespoke options.

owner_reviews.summary: Edmunds and KBB do not publish meaningful owner-review aggregates for the Flying Spur due to small US owner population and limited consumer submissions.

EPA shows combined gas MPG 19 (city/highway split not separately published for the PHEV). Bentley does not publish US MSRP per spec §13. NHTSA/IIHS untested per spec §13. All-wheel steering is standard across the Flying Spur range, providing a tighter turning circle at low speeds and improved stability at high speeds. MSRP source: MotorTrend editorial spec page (per §4.6 scoped policy; manufacturer does not publish US MSRP).

models.notes: Flying Spur transitioned to all-PHEV powertrain for 2025/2026 - the prior W12 6.0L and pure 4.0L V8 are discontinued. Four trims for 2026; two power tunes (HPH 671 bhp on base/Azure; UPH 771 bhp on Speed/Mulliner). Per spec §13 ultra-luxury, Bentley does not publish US MSRP - msrp_base/destination_fee null across all trims. NHTSA and IIHS have not crash-tested the Flying Spur. EPA fueleconomy.gov publishes a single ID (49754) for the Flying Spur line. All 4 trims have their own trim_family per sole-trim atomic rule with distinct wheels/grille/badging. All-wheel steering is standard across the lineup. Curb weight ~5,820 lb across trims; PHEV battery adds ~440 lb vs prior W12 sedan.

models.generation_context: Second-generation Bentayga (2G), introduced 2020, refreshed 2022 with new front fascia, oval taillamps, and modernized infotainment. Standard wheelbase configuration with two powertrain options: 4.0L twin-turbo V8 (550 PS / 542 bhp) on most trims, or 3.0L V6 plug-in hybrid (462 PS / 456 bhp combined). The performance-focused Bentayga Speed exclusively uses a higher-output 650 PS (641 bhp) tune of the V8.
models.model_summary: Bentley's ultra-luxury SUV in standard wheelbase form. Five trims for 2026: Bentayga (V8 base), Bentayga Hybrid (V6 PHEV), Bentayga Speed (V8 performance), Bentayga Mulliner (V8 luxury pinnacle), and Bentayga Azure (V8 wellness). Available in 4-seat, 4+1-seat, and 5-seat configurations. Features 48V Bentley Dynamic Ride active anti-roll, all-wheel steering, and 8-speed automatic across all trims.

reliability.summary: JD Power VDS and Consumer Reports do not meaningfully sample Bentley due to very low US sales volume. No quantitative reliability rating is available.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power APEAL does not publish scores for Bentley because of low US sales volume.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the 2026 Bentayga for retaining the Bentley signature blend of luxury and surprising agility for a 5,400+ lb SUV, citing the 48V active anti-roll, all-wheel steering, and the new 650 PS Bentayga Speed's 3.4-sec 0-60 (quickest Bentayga ever). The V6 hybrid is positioned as the conscience-easing option with 25 miles EV-only range. Critiques focus on the relatively short PHEV range (vs newer luxury SUV competitors), the still-extreme weight, and the perception of an aging interior layout despite the 2022 refresh.

owner_reviews.summary: Edmunds and KBB do not publish meaningful owner-review aggregates for the Bentayga due to small US owner population and limited consumer submissions.

trims.notes: Sole-trim family: Bentayga (V8) - is_base_trim:true of the V8 ICE powertrain line per spec §13. Powertrain.type 'ice' (pure ICE, no electrification). EPA fueleconomy.gov ID 49562 verified specifically for 2026 Bentley Bentayga V8. Bentley does not publish US MSRP per spec §13. NHTSA/IIHS untested. Towing capacity 7,716 lb (3,500 kg) per Bentley manufacturer spec. Curb weight from US-market figure (~5,318 lb / 2,412 kg); EU figure is 2,412 kg. Bentayga base offers a 4-seat config (with center console), 4+1, or 5-seat configuration. MSRP source: MotorTrend editorial spec page (per §4.6 scoped policy; manufacturer does not publish US MSRP).

All-wheel steering standard

trims.notes: Sole-trim family: Bentayga Hybrid (V6 PHEV) - is_base_trim:true of the PHEV powertrain line per spec §13 multi-powertrain rule (cheapest of each line is is_base_trim:true). Powertrain.type 'phev' (plug-in hybrid). EPA fueleconomy.gov ID 49751 verified specifically for 2026 Bentley Bentayga Hybrid. EPA shows combined gas MPG 21 (city/highway split not separately published for the PHEV; 21 applied to all three slots). EPA electric range 20 mi (vs 25 mi WLTP cited by Bentley). MPGe 42 combined. Bentley does not publish US MSRP. NHTSA/IIHS untested. Bentley markets this on bentleymotors.com as a powertrain option within the base Bentayga page; structured as its own trim per multi-powertrain rule with own EPA listing. MSRP source: MotorTrend editorial spec page (per §4.6 scoped policy; manufacturer does not publish US MSRP).

trims.notes: Sole-trim family: Bentayga Speed. is_base_trim:true with delta_from_base:null per sole-trim atomic rule and spec §13 separate-powertrain-line rule (650 PS / 641 bhp performance tune is its own line, different higher-output engine vs 550 PS base V8 with larger injectors, lower compression, higher boost). Quickest Bentayga ever (0-60 in 3.4 sec). Top speed 193 mph with optional carbon ceramic brakes. EPA fueleconomy.gov ID 49562 cited per spec §4 EPA-unavailable rule - EPA does not publish separate Speed-trim entries; mechanically same fuel economy band as V8 base. Bentley does not publish US MSRP. NHTSA/IIHS untested. MSRP not findable from allowed editorial sources as of 2026-05-15.

trims.notes: Sole-trim family: Bentayga Mulliner. is_base_trim:true with delta_from_base:null per sole-trim atomic rule. Mulliner is listed as a current Bentayga model variant on bentleymotors.com/en/models.html, with own Mulliner-specific image set and 22-inch Paragon wheels. The dedicated /bentayga-mulliner.html URL on the manufacturer's site currently redirects to a heritage page documenting the prior W12-era Mulliner SWB (2017-2018); Bentley markets the current Bentayga Mulliner under the parent bentleymotors.com/en/models/bentayga.html range page as a trim choice. Mulliner pairs the standard 542 bhp V8 with bespoke trim, badging, and wheel set; V6 PHEV also configurable. Bentley does not publish US MSRP. NHTSA/IIHS untested. Powertrain.type 'ice' (V8 base configuration documented; V6 PHEV available as alternative). MSRP not findable from allowed editorial sources as of 2026-05-15.

trims.notes: Sole-trim family: Bentayga Azure. is_base_trim:true with delta_from_base:null per sole-trim atomic rule. Azure has its own model card on bentleymotors.com with unique 22-inch polished wheels, wellness-focused massage seats, PM2.5 cabin air filtration, and chrome vertical-vane grille. Available with either 542 bhp V8 (default in this entry) or 456 bhp V6 PHEV powertrain. Bentley does not publish US MSRP. NHTSA/IIHS untested. Powertrain.type 'ice' reflects the V8 base configuration; V6 PHEV configurable as alternative. MSRP source: MotorTrend editorial spec page (per §4.6 scoped policy; manufacturer does not publish US MSRP).

models.notes: Bentayga (standard wheelbase) has five trims for 2026, spanning two distinct powertrain lines per spec §13 multi-powertrain rule: V8 ICE (base/Mulliner/Azure/Speed) and V6 PHEV (Hybrid). Speed gets its own powertrain line as the higher-output 650 PS V8 tune is a distinct calibration with own intake, injectors, lowered compression, higher boost. Mulliner and Azure can also be ordered with the V6 hybrid; the V8 configuration is documented here as primary with V6 PHEV noted as alternative. All 5 trims have their own trim_family (each with distinct wheels/grille/badging) so each is is_base_trim:true with delta_from_base:null per sole-trim atomic rule. EPA fueleconomy.gov has separate IDs for V8 Bentayga (49562) and V6 Hybrid (49751) - verified individually. Per spec §13 ultra-luxury, Bentley does not publish US MSRP. NHTSA and IIHS have not crash-tested Bentayga (low-volume luxury SUV). The Bentayga Artenara Edition (limited-edition tribute to original Bentley Mulsanne) is treated as a package per spec §6.5 rather than a separate trim because Bentley markets it as an edition of the Mulliner/Azure variants and only ~50 units are being built worldwide.

models.generation_context: Extended-wheelbase variant of the second-generation Bentayga (2G EWB), introduced 2022 with a 7.1-inch longer wheelbase (180 mm) compared to the standard Bentayga to deliver chauffeur-grade rear-seat accommodation. Three trims for 2026: Bentayga EWB (base), Bentayga EWB Mulliner (luxury pinnacle), and Bentayga EWB Azure (wellness). All EWB trims use the 4.0L twin-turbo V8 (550 PS / 542 bhp) - no V6 PHEV option, no Speed performance variant.
models.model_summary: Long-wheelbase chauffeur-focused Bentley Bentayga, sharing the 4.0L V8 twin-turbo powertrain with the standard Bentayga but adding 7.1 inches of wheelbase entirely to rear-seat space. Available with 4-seat (Airline Seat Specification with reclining individual rear thrones) or 5-seat configuration. Three trims for 2026 - all V8-only as there is no V6 PHEV or Speed variant offered in EWB form for 2026 MY. Body style retains suv-midsize classification per project taxonomy.

reliability.summary: JD Power VDS and Consumer Reports do not meaningfully sample Bentley due to very low US sales volume. No quantitative reliability rating is available.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power APEAL does not publish scores for Bentley because of low US sales volume.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers describe the Bentayga EWB as the most luxurious SUV money can buy when configured with the 4-seat Airline Seat Specification, citing the 22-way adjustable rear thrones with sleep mode and the dramatically improved rear-seat space. Praise centers on the rear-seat experience, the unflappable air-suspension ride, and bespoke craftsmanship. Critiques focus on the EWB-exclusive V8-only powertrain (no PHEV option), the considerable curb weight, and the smaller cargo area (380 L vs 484 L in SWB) due to the extended wheelbase chassis architecture.

owner_reviews.summary: Edmunds and KBB do not publish meaningful owner-review aggregates for the Bentayga EWB due to small US owner population and limited consumer submissions.

trims.notes: Sole-trim family: Bentayga EWB base. is_base_trim:true with delta_from_base:null per sole-trim atomic rule. Powertrain.type 'ice' (no PHEV variant offered in EWB). EPA fueleconomy.gov ID 49563 verified specifically for 2026 Bentley Bentayga EWB V8. Bentley does not publish US MSRP per spec §13. NHTSA/IIHS untested. EWB adds 7.1 inches (180 mm) of wheelbase over standard Bentayga, all going to rear-seat space. Boot capacity reduced to 13.42 cu-ft (380 L vs 484 L in SWB) due to extended chassis architecture and Privacy Suite glass partition stowage on the optional configuration. Available with 4-seat Airline Seat Specification (22-way reclining individual thrones), 4+1, or 5-seat configurations. MSRP source: MotorTrend editorial spec page (per §4.6 scoped policy; manufacturer does not publish US MSRP).

trims.notes: Sole-trim family: Bentayga EWB Mulliner. is_base_trim:true with delta_from_base:null per sole-trim atomic rule. Bentley positions this as the pinnacle Bentayga model and as their flagship 4-seat chauffeur-spec SUV (rivaling Rolls-Royce Cullinan and Maybach GLS 600). Mulliner spec includes 4-seat Airline Seat Specification with 22-way reclining individual rear thrones, optional Privacy Suite glass partition, twin 10-inch rear entertainment screens, and Mulliner-bespoke duo-tone veneers. EPA fueleconomy.gov ID 49563 cited per spec §4 EPA-unavailable rule - shared with EWB base. V8 powertrain only (no PHEV variant offered in EWB Mulliner). Bentley does not publish US MSRP. NHTSA/IIHS untested. Capacity set to 4 reflecting standard 4-seat config. MSRP source: MotorTrend editorial spec page (per §4.6 scoped policy; manufacturer does not publish US MSRP).

trims.notes: Sole-trim family: Bentayga EWB Azure. is_base_trim:true with delta_from_base:null per sole-trim atomic rule. Azure has its own model card on bentleymotors.com with unique 22-inch polished wheels, wellness suite (massage + PM2.5 air filtration), and chrome vertical-vane grille. V8 powertrain only in EWB Azure. EPA fueleconomy.gov ID 49563 shared per spec §4 EPA-unavailable rule. Bentley does not publish US MSRP. NHTSA/IIHS untested. MSRP source: MotorTrend editorial spec page (per §4.6 scoped policy; manufacturer does not publish US MSRP).

models.notes: Bentayga EWB (extended-wheelbase) has 3 trims for 2026: base, Mulliner, Azure - all V8 only (no V6 PHEV variant, no Speed performance variant offered in EWB form). 180 mm (7.1 in) longer wheelbase than standard Bentayga adds entirely to rear-seat space. Per spec §13 ultra-luxury, Bentley does not publish US MSRP - msrp_base/destination_fee null across all trims. NHTSA and IIHS have not crash-tested Bentayga EWB. EPA fueleconomy.gov publishes a single ID (49563) for EWB. All 3 trims have their own trim_family per sole-trim atomic rule. Cargo volume reduced to 380 L (13.42 cu-ft) vs 484 L (17.1 cu-ft) in SWB due to extended chassis architecture. Body style retained as suv-midsize per project taxonomy. Excluded: Bentayga EWB Artenara Edition (treated as a limited-edition package per spec §6.5), Bentayga EWB Chalet Edition (May 2026 announced Mulliner-bespoke commission with limited production).
